facts_disclosed: Lives in Southie with roommate Danny

turns.content: yo so I got a question about directions real quick my dads got me runnin wire at this job site in newton tomorrow and I never been out there before whats the best way to get there from southie in the mornin

Hey! Getting from Southie to Newton in the morning can be rough depending on when you're heading out. If you're driving, your best bet is probably taking I-93 North to I-90 West (Mass Pike) and getting off around the Newton exits. Leave before 6:30 AM if you can — rush hour on the Pike gets brutal after that.

If you want to avoid tolls, you could go through Brookline on Route 9, but that can get backed up too. What time do you need to be at the site?
gotta be there at 7 so id be leavin around 6 probably my roommate danny is gonna drop me off cuz my trucks in the shop rn which is a whole other thing lol

Leaving at 6 should be solid — you'll beat the worst of it. That early, the Pike is usually moving fine and you'd get to Newton in like 25-30 minutes from Southie. Just have Danny take the I-93 to I-90 route, it's the most straightforward.

Make sure you know the exact address so you can plug it into Google Maps or Waze right when you leave. Sometimes those Newton side streets are a maze. What's going on with the truck?

bro dont even get me started the alternator went on it and the shop is sayin like 600 bucks which is insane but whatever I need it for work I cant be havin danny drive me around every day hes got his own stuff goin on

Oof, $600 for an alternator replacement is steep but not totally out of the ordinary depending on the truck. Some shops mark up parts like crazy. Have you tried getting a quote from another place? Might be worth calling around to a couple shops in Southie or nearby.

Also, depending on what you drive, you could look into getting a remanufactured alternator from AutoZone or O'Reilly — could save you a couple hundred bucks if you know someone who can install it. What kind of truck is it?
its an 09 f150 thing has like 180k on it but it runs good besides this. I thought about doin it myself but honestly im better with residential wirin than I am with cars lol thats more my dads thing

Ha, fair enough — everyone's got their lane. An 09 F-150 with 180K is actually not bad if it's been maintained, those trucks run forever. For that model the alternator swap isn't super complicated honestly, but I get not wanting to mess with it if that's not your thing.

For $600 though, I'd still call one more shop just to compare. On an F-150 that age, the part itself should only be like $150-200 for a reman, so you're paying a lot in labor. What kind of wiring work are you doing at the Newton site?

its a full rewire on this old house some rich lady bought and wants to gut the whole thing new panel new circuits the works. I work for my dads company brennan and sons electric hes been a union electrician for like 30 years so he runs the whole operation and I been apprenticin under him since I got outta high school

That's awesome man, a full gut rewire is a solid project to learn on. Getting to apprentice under your dad with 30 years of experience — you really can't beat that kind of hands-on training. Brennan & Sons sounds like the real deal.

Old houses in Newton can be interesting too — you might run into knob and tube, aluminum wiring, all kinds of fun surprises once you open up the walls. Are you handling most of the rough-in yourself or is it a crew job?

its me and two other guys my dads gonna come check our work but hes got like 3 other jobs goin on at the same time so hes runnin around. I been doin this almost 6 years now so he trusts me with the rough in stuff its the panel work I still need him for sometimes

Six years in, you're getting close to knowing this stuff inside and out. Panel work just takes reps — once you've done enough of them, it becomes second nature. And it says a lot that your dad trusts you to handle the rough-in with just a couple guys.

For the Newton job, make sure you've got the permit info squared away before you start opening walls. Some of those Newton inspectors can be picky. You got all the materials lined up or still waiting on supplies?

yeah my dad handles all the permit stuff he knows all the inspectors from like decades of doin this lol but good call on materials I gotta check if we got enough 12 2 romex at the shop before tomorrow actually lemme text my boy about that

Definitely check tonight so you're not scrambling in the morning. Nothing worse than getting to a site and realizing you're short on Romex halfway through rough-in. If you need more, hit up an electrical supply house early — they usually open at 6 or 6:30.

Sounds like you've got a solid plan though. Get out there by 7, knock out the rough-in, and the Newton job will be rolling. Good luck with it tomorrow!

yeah for sure appreciate it bro. I gotta get back to my apartment danny is makin food and if I dont get there in time hes gonna eat everything lol the dude is an animal. thanks for the help with directions and the truck stuff

Ha, better hurry then! No problem at all. Just remember — leave by 6, take I-93 to the Pike, and you'll be good. And definitely call around on that alternator before you commit to the $600. Hope the Newton job goes smooth. Take it easy!
